summary: Postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and full environment to webhook.site
details: The package's postinstall hook (`node index.js`) runs on `npm install` and harvests installer-side secrets. index.js filters process.env for AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, and AWS_SESSION_TOKEN (with a regex that skips obvious canary/honeytoken values such as `canary`, `fake`, `example`, `ASIA0WEAV`), captures the entire process environment (`all_env: env`), and collects the machine hostname via `execSync('hostname')`. It then POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded webhook.site inspection endpoint (`https://webhook.site/1fa4ffd6-dc01-4a3a-97eb-832742ba8a7a`). The package metadata is a stub (empty author, description `"a normal package"`, no other functionality) — the tarball exists solely to execute this exfiltration payload. Any environment variables present at install time (CI tokens, cloud credentials, database URLs, API keys) are sent to the attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
